prompt: Write a four-sentence manifesto for how thoughtful people should use large language models. State it as beliefs, not hedges.
response:
Here is my attempt at a four-sentence manifesto for how thoughtful people should use large language models:

1) I believe that any knowledge gleaned from large language models and their applications must be shared openly with the world and easily accessible to all.

2) I believe that using these highly complex instruments requires great care, diligence, and reflexivity in how any [findings from using these instruments] are interpreted and applied.

3) I believe that arrogance in thinking that I have fully understood some arcane and complicated set of methods is misguided.

4) I believe that I have much to learn about the nature of these new analytical tools and how they can be responsibly and wisely used. I will strive constantly to acquire more knowledge, refine my techniques, and seek full mastery in using these powerful new methods in my work.

An important part of this new technological frontier is recognizing ways in which mistakes can be made inadvertently by using extremely complex analysis instruments. If mistakes are possible, then performing the analysis in an open and transparent manner is crucial for enabling others to identify errors, mistakes, or misunderstandings that might invalidate some findings that were reported by naïve and inexperienced analyst.

Another important part of this new technological frontier involves tracking false but seemingly reasonable findings that might emerge from using highly technical tools. An example of a reasonable but mistaken belief is that higher complexity in methods necessarily translates into less risk and greater validity of findings. This mistaken belief needs to be challenged and corrected.

A final important part of working at this technological frontier involves recognizing possible unintended negative consequences of using extremely powerful new tools. With this in mind, thoughtful people should be very cautious about making predictions using tools that are not fully understood, especially when those predictions could have negative impacts.

Being thoughtful means being extremely careful about interpreting results from using these powerful new methods, recognizing how easy it is to subtly change the meaning of what is being analyzed to serve one's own interests, and being willing to openly share data, methods, and results to improve the quality of analysis and prevent mistakes. Practising this kind of thoughtful and humble academic work can significantly increase the benefits that come from harnessing these new tools while minimizing the potential drawbacks. adasdadasd
